children.label: Intro Circles can create enough user value and business viability to justify further investment
Onboarding creates enough expected or immediate value that target users grant the required access and build a useful Intro Circle
The promised Intro Circles value is strong and trusted enough that target users grant the required access
children.test: In live onboarding, measure the share of target users who grant the required access after seeing the value proposition and candidate circle flow. Pass if at least 60% of target users who start onboarding grant the required access.

children.label: Onboarding reveals enough expected or immediate value that target users add or invite enough relevant people to build a useful Intro Circle
The system surfaces enough relevant people for the user to build a useful Intro Circle during onboarding
children.test: In live onboarding, show suggested people from user data and measure how many suggested people are selected into the Intro Circle. Pass if at least 50% of users who reach the suggestion step select at least 3 suggested people.

children.label: Users are willing to add or invite enough relevant people during onboarding
children.test: In live onboarding, measure how many users actually add or invite enough people to form an initial Intro Circle. Pass if at least 40% of users who granted access add or invite at least 5 people.

children.label: A circle built this way is large and relevant enough to generate useful intro opportunities
children.test: In live onboarding, measure how many users build a circle that reaches the predefined usable-circle threshold. Pass if at least 35% of users who complete onboarding build a circle that meets the usable-circle threshold.

children.label: Users retain Intro Circles for long enough for value to deepen over time
The main ongoing value paths together provide enough recurring value across user situations to support retention
For enough users, the core intro workflow reliably turns circle-member meetings into successful intro opportunities often enough to create recurring value
The system can identify relevant meeting-triggered intro opportunities inside the circle
children.test: Run automated opportunity detection on live user data and measure how often surfaced opportunities lead to entry into the opportunity flow. Pass if at least 20% of surfaced opportunities lead to a user opening the opportunity flow, and at least 25% of active users receive at least 1 surfaced opportunity in a 30-day period.

children.label: Users ask for introductions often enough when Intro Circles surfaces relevant opportunities
Users engage with the surfaced intro opportunities email enough to enter the opportunity flow
children.test: Live intro-opportunity emails: measure the share of delivered emails that produce a click into the opportunity flow. Pass if at least 25% of delivered intro-opportunity emails produce a click into the opportunity flow.

children.label: Users select surfaced intro opportunities and send actual intro requests often enough
children.test: Live surfaced opportunities: measure the share of surfaced opportunities that are selected by the user and result in a real intro request being sent. Pass if at least 20% of surfaced opportunities lead to a real intro request being sent.

children.label: Circle members complete enough requested introductions for the core workflow to create value
Circle members notice and understand the intro request email
children.test: Measure how often middle people respond to, acknowledge, or act on the intro request email. Pass if at least 50% of intro-request emails receive any response or explicit acknowledgement.

children.label: Circle members agree to make the intro often enough when asked
children.test: For real intro requests sent to circle members, measure how often the middle person agrees to help. Pass if at least 30% of intro requests sent to middle people get a positive agreement to make the intro.

children.label: Agreed intro requests turn into actual completed introductions often enough
children.test: Among intro requests where the middle person agreed, measure how often a real intro is completed. Pass if at least 70% of agreed intro requests become completed intros within 14 days.

children.label: For enough users or meetings where intro opportunities are not the main value, at least one complementary email workflow provides enough positive value
The pre-meeting brief email provides enough positive value in meetings where intro opportunities are not relevant or not the main reason to use the product
children.test: In live operation, measure how often users take a downstream action from the brief. Pass if at least 20% of delivered brief emails lead to a downstream action such as clicking through or using a suggested action.

children.label: For retained users, Intro Circles has at least one credible way for value to deepen over time
The product can expand already-usable Intro Circles over time enough to materially deepen value
The product surfaces enough relevant additional people after onboarding for users to expand an already-usable Intro Circle
children.test: In normal post-onboarding prompts, measure how many suggested people users select as additions to their circle. Pass if at least 30% of prompted users select at least 1 suggested person.

children.label: Users add or invite enough relevant people after onboarding to expand an already-usable Intro Circle
children.test: In normal post-onboarding use, measure how many users actually add or invite suggested people after receiving expansion prompts. Pass if at least 20% of prompted users add or invite at least 1 person.

children.label: Later circle expansion materially increases value for users who already have a usable Intro Circle
children.test: Compare downstream opportunity generation before vs. after users expand their circle. Pass if users who expand their circle increase surfaced opportunities per active month by at least 50%.

children.label: Distribution economics can work for Intro Circles
Onboarding-stage circle invitations can economically acquire activated users
Repeat-meeting circle invitations can economically acquire activated users
Direct outbound intro campaigns can economically acquire activated users
Service economics can work for Intro Circles
The ongoing cost to deliver Intro Circles value can stay low enough per active user
Heavier Intro Circles users do not break the service economics
Value capture can work for Intro Circles
A focused Intro Circles product can capture enough value from users
Intro Circles can lead enough users into a broader paid relationship product
The reachable market can support a business around Intro Circles
There are enough reachable target users for the initial Intro Circles wedge
The reachable target users have enough relevant meeting and intro frequency for Intro Circles to matter
The reachable target users are network-dense enough for Intro Circles to spread and deliver value
